text.title: Agency Niche Positioning Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Agency Niche Positioning application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Define niche as a single vertical (industry + buyer + use-case), not a horizontal capability ('Shopify dev for DTC supplement brands', not 'e-commerce dev').
rule.rationale: Anti-fallback: horizontal positioning collapses to 'we do everything' under sales pressure; verticals survive.

rule.statement: Niche statement passes the discomfort test: founder feels it 'leaves money on the table'. If comfortable, narrow further.
rule.rationale: Philip Morgan, The Positioning Manual: comfortable niches are too broad to compound authority.

rule.statement: Write a 1-page refusal rubric: out-of-niche industries, out-of-niche project shapes, budget floor. Documented BEFORE next inbound arrives.
rule.rationale: Pre-commitment removes founder-discretion during opportunistic moments.

rule.statement: Public portfolio top 3 case studies match the chosen niche. Off-niche moved to 'archived' subsection.
rule.rationale: Buyers verify positioning via portfolio in <30 seconds; mismatch kills trust.

rule.statement: After 90 days, count: inbound matching niche / total inbound. If <40%, niche statement, rubric, OR channel is wrong. Revise ONE variable.
rule.rationale: Feedback loop guards against premature commitment + premature abandonment.
